text.title: plg-optimization-tactics Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for plg-optimization-tactics as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: LLMs recommend "remove all limits" or "give 30-day trial" — this conflicts with the balanced-tier guidance.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Pin the system prompt to the specific limit-type matrix in this methodology.

failure-mode.description: Auto-generated upgrade copy drifts to generic SaaS language ("powerful features", "enterprise-grade").
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Add a post-filter that scans for these tokens and rewrites them using named customers and quantified value.

failure-mode.description: Agents writing directly to a paid Stripe price object or live Appcues flow.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Gate every mutation behind a human-approved PR or ticket.

failure-mode.description: Free-tier limit changes shipped autonomously by agent with high ICE score.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Free-tier limit changes must be reviewed with finance + retention data — never let an agent ship a limit change autonomously.

failure-mode.description: PQL scoring weights reused from last quarter's coefficients without re-fitting.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Require the agent to re-fit weights against converted cohorts every quarter.

failure-mode.description: "Empty State Time under 2 minutes" target used without server-side data seeding step in the onboarding flow spec.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agents producing onboarding flows must explicitly include the seed step or the metric is unreachable.
